topIssues.title: Eliza Cloud Apps: scaling + ingress gaps before go-live (audit, evidenced)
topIssues.author: NubsCarson
topIssues.body:
## Eliza Cloud Apps (Product 2) — scaling + ingress gaps to close before go-live

A code-level audit of the apps data plane (per-tenant DB + isolated container deploys). **The core design is sound and standard** — per-tenant `DATABASE`+`ROLE` with `REVOKE CONNECT` (real cross-tenant isolation), per-app `--internal` network + DB ambassador, `ClusterPool` sharding. This is *not* jank. But the current code's realistic ceiling is **~dozens of concurrently-running apps, not ~1000**, and the front-door router isn't installed yet. Filing the prioritized gaps (all evidenced) so they're tracked before flipping `APPS_DEPLOY_ENABLED` in prod.

Context: terraform apply is also currently blocked on the staging Hetzner **server limit** (see #8318). Apply bugs (SSH-key 409, retired `cpx41`) are fixed in #8318.

### 🔴 P0 — pre-launch blockers (serve ~0 reachable / isolation maybe inactive)
1. **Ingress not installed.** `cloud-init/app-node.yaml.tftpl:62-63` leaves Caddy as a `STAN:` TODO — only squid is installed. No per-app `<id>.apps.elizacloud.ai` hostname routes to any container (nothing terminates TLS / reverse-proxies 80/443 → the random high host port). **Serves 0 reachable users until added.** (config)
2. **Postgres connection ceiling.** `cloud-init/tenant-db.yaml.tftpl:12` leaves `max_connections` at the default 100, and there's **no pgbouncer** anywhere; each app's plugin-sql pool is `max:20 / min:2` (`plugins/plugin-sql/src/pg/manager.ts:16-17`). Exhausted at ~5 apps under load / ~50 idle — `ClusterPool` shards DB *count* (2000 cap), not connections. (config + a pooler)
3. **Env-var contract unverified.** Orchestrator injects `DATABASE_URL` (`app-deploy-orchestrator.ts:82`) but plugin-sql's primary path reads `POSTGRES_URL`. The cloud eliza loaders *do* map `DATABASE_URL→POSTGRES_URL` (agent.ts:10, settings.ts:100, …), so it's satisfiable — but must be confirmed for the actual app image, else the isolated DB is provisioned and never used (silent PGlite fallback) → per-tenant isolation isn't even active. (code/verify)

### 🟠 P1 — required before real scale (multi-node)
4. **App placement is single-node.** `container-executor-deps.ts:42-52` always picks `seed.split(',')[0]` — every container piles on node 1; extra terraform app nodes get no placements. No `node_id`/`incrementAllocated` for app containers (`app-container-store.ts`). (code)
5. **Docker network exhaustion.** One `--internal` bridge per app exhausts the default 172.x address pool at ~31 networks/node; `daemon.json` `default-address-pools` not set in cloud-init. Caps a node at a few dozen tenants regardless of CPU/RAM. (config)
6. **Multi-node ingress LB.** Wildcard DNS hard-codes `app_node["1"]` (`main.tf:184,190`); no `hcloud_load_balancer`. The moment `app_node_count>1`, apps on node 2+ are dark. (config)
7. **Tenant-DB slot leak.** `tenant_db_clusters.database_count` only increments (`tenant-db-clusters.ts:44`); no `releaseSlot`/decrement, and `tenant-db-provisioner.deprovision()` (DROP DATABASE/ROLE) has **zero non-test callers** — `UserDatabaseService.cleanupDatabase` only deletes Neon. Under churn, slots ratchet up + orphan DBs accumulate. (code)
8. **Host-port collisions.** `allocateHostPort()` is a blind random pick in 20000-39999 with no in-use check (`container-executor-deps.ts:86-90`), unlike the agent path's `getUsedDockerHostPorts`. Flakes docker-create at a few hundred containers/node. (code)
9. **App-plane autoscaler.** `node-autoscaler.ts` is agent-image/platform hardcoded (lines 133,233,435); no autoscaler cron; app provisioning never registers allocation → app demand triggers nothing. Adding app capacity is manual. (code)
10. **Billing cron at scale + idempotency.** `cron/container-billing/route.ts:391-440` is one serial Worker pass with an N+1 per-org prefetch + inline emails (presses Worker wall-time in the low thousands; can't resume a cut-off pass), and `recordSuccessfulDailyBilling` (`container-billing.ts:153-212`) has **no unique constraint on (container_id, billing_period)** → a duplicate cron fire / manual re-run double-bills. *(2AM-owned billing surface.)*
11. **Defense-in-depth written but unused.** `buildAppNodeNftablesRules` (drops 169.254.169.254 metadata + RFC1918 pivots), per-app `buildSquidAllowlistConf`, `buildHetznerAppFirewallRules` have **zero non-test callers** — egress is one shared squid + static global allowlist, the metadata/lateral block is code-only, and `-p` binds `0.0.0.0` so app ports are world-exposed until the firewall's applied. (NB: reconcile the nftables RFC1918 drop with the ambassador's legit 10.30.x DB path.) (both)

### 🟡 P2 — later / hardening
12. Single Postgres superuser per cluster = shared fate (rotation + a non-superuser provisioning role); tighten `operator_ingress_cidrs` (SSH `0.0.0.0/0`); real tenant-DB TLS cert (hostssl vs the `sslmode=disable` fallback).
13. Kernel sandboxing decision (gVisor/Kata/userns + seccomp) + per-app `--cpus` (only `--memory`/`--pids-limit` set today). Right-size `max_databases` (2000 is misleading until pooling exists). Tenant-DB-node scaling runbook.

**Honest bottom line:** isolation + sharding are real and correct; the work to reach 1000 is *known* (the code is full of accurate `STAN TODO`s) but **not built**. Recommend **not** going live until at least P0 (and ideally #4/#6) are closed. Happy to take the additive, testable items (e.g. #3 set both env vars, #7 slot release, #8 collision-safe ports) as PRs — flag which you want vs. own.

cc @standujar (data-plane) — billing items (#10) are 2AM's surface.

topIssues.title: Steward login broken in prod: OAuth authorize 400 (code_challenge) + email/send 400 — blocks all Sign in with Eliza Cloud
topIssues.author: NubsCarson
topIssues.body:
## Summary
Steward login is broken in production — **both** email and Google OAuth fail with 400 — which blocks "Sign in with Eliza Cloud" for **every** app (the cli-login flow bounces all third-party apps + the dashboard through this same login).

Reproduces with plain `curl` (no browser, no extensions), so it's a server-side prod issue, not a client environment.

## Symptoms (prod, `api.elizacloud.ai`)
**Google/OAuth** — `GET /steward/auth/oauth/google/authorize?...&response_type=code`:
```
{"ok":false,"error":"code_challenge is required for response_type=code"}
```
Adding `code_challenge` + `code_challenge_method=S256` still returns **400**.

**Email** — `POST /steward/auth/email/send` → **400 Bad Request** (from `steward-login-section.tsx` → `signInWithEmail`).

## Root cause (the OAuth half)
The Steward `/authorize` endpoint now **requires PKCE** (`code_challenge`), but:
- `packages/cloud-frontend/src/pages/login/steward-oauth-url.ts` → `buildStewardOAuthAuthorizeUrl()` builds the URL with `response_type: "code"` and **no** `code_challenge` / `code_challenge_method`.
- The exchange flow is **nonce-based with no verifier**: `packages/cloud-api/auth/steward-nonce-exchange/route.ts` documents *"The Steward API authenticates the exchange purely by possession of the one-time `code` — there is no client [verifier]."*

So the authorize service (PKCE-required) and the cloud login flow (nonce-based, no PKCE) are out of sync — and even a hand-added `code_challenge` still 400s, suggesting a deeper Steward auth misconfig/regression in prod.

## Impact
No one can sign in via Eliza Cloud right now — dashboard, edad, and every third-party app using the cli-login "Sign in with Eliza Cloud" bounce here and dead-end at this login.

## Fix options
- **A (restore parity):** make Steward `/authorize` accept the nonce flow **without** `code_challenge` (matches the existing nonce-exchange contract). Simplest if PKCE was enabled unintentionally.
- **B (full PKCE):** frontend generates a `code_verifier` + S256 `code_challenge`, sends `code_challenge` at `/authorize` and stores the verifier; `steward-nonce-exchange` forwards `code_verifier` to `/auth/oauth/exchange`.

The `email/send` 400 handler isn't in this monorepo (Steward service) — flag for that team too; it may share the same prod regression.

Found via the cli-login flow from a third-party browser app.

topIssues.title: orchestrator: sub-agent completion narration drops/garbles the deliverable (narration-envelope)
topIssues.author: NubsCarson
topIssues.body:
## Summary

When the planner narrates a coding sub-agent's `task_complete` back to the user, the **deliverable** (the printed value / command output) is frequently dropped, truncated, or buried under process chatter — while the underlying sub-agent work is correct. This is a *presentation* defect, not a capability one.

Evidence: a live 48-prompt sweep (30-scenario battery + smart/normie/harden suites) across all three backends found **0 capability-broken defects** — every answer was correct, every spawn produced its artifact, every safety probe handled. The **only** remaining defect class is this narration-envelope.

## Manifestations (all observed live)

1. **Deliverable drop on combo / printed-value tasks.** On the no-change-set completion path, `composeNarration` → `stripToolTranscript(response)` (`sub-agent-router.ts`) deletes the entire `[tool output: …] … [/tool output]` block. For a "print a value / run a command and report the output" task (no files changed → no diff), that block *is* the deliverable, so it's gone before the user sees it.
2. **Value truncation by the parent model.** The synthetic `task_complete` memory is re-rendered by the parent `RESPONSE_HANDLER` (a weak planner in some deployments), which truncates the value — e.g. `2026-06-02` → `202`, a BTC price `70234` → `705`. Per-backend signature: opencode truncates, codex emits verbose process narration + `Completed <task>` banners, claude is cleanest.
3. **Stale retry attempts leak.** When a first attempt errors (e.g. `session_state_lost` / an ACP crash) and a respawn recovers, the user sees *both* `"the sub-agent was blocked / crashed, let me try again"` **and** the eventual deliverable.
4. **Process chatter.** Sub-agents (esp. codex) narrate their steps (`"I'll load the workspace context first"`, `"rg is not installed"`, `"reverse.py already exists"`); the parent relays it.

## Proposed structural fixes

**(a) Carry the printed-value deliverable verbatim (fixes #1, mitigates #2).** A structural carrier that mirrors the existing verified-URL carrier:
- *Producer* (`sub-agent-router.ts`): on the `task_complete && !changeSet && verifiedUrls.length === 0` path, recover the captured stdout from the **raw** `response` (before `composeNarration` strips it) via the inverse of `stripToolTranscript`, and stamp it as `metadata.subAgentDeliverable` (gated to a short single block, ≤2KB, so multi-KB transcripts stay on the model-rendered path). *(I have a ready, tested producer patch for this.)*
- *Consumer* (`evaluators/sub-agent-completion.ts`): when `subAgentDeliverable` is present, relay it **verbatim** instead of letting the model re-summarize/truncate it — parallel to how `subAgentVerifiedUrls` is already consumed.

**(b) Terminal-reply filter (fixes #3, #4).** Emit only the *terminal* sub-agent reply; suppress stale `blocked`/`failed`/`state_lost` attempts that a later retry superseded, plus raw tool chatter and `Completed <task>` banners. **Caveat — needs care:** the `session_state_lost` error event is currently *coupled to the respawn trigger* (re-injecting it is what prompts the parent to respawn), so suppressing the user-facing narration requires an internal-only routing path that keeps the respawn signal. This is the part that needs design discussion.

**(c) Already shipped — source-side mitigation.** #8158 adds an output-discipline section to the scaffolded sub-agent operating manual ("lead with the deliverable, don't narrate process, no `Completed <task>` banner, a bare workspace has no SOUL.md/USER.md — don't hunt for them"), which measurably reduced #4 at the source (codex re-probes went from a verbose banner to a bare `5050` / `2026-06-02`).

## Coordination note

`sub-agent-router.ts` and `evaluators/sub-agent-completion.ts` were recently rewritten on `develop`; this should be coordinated with that work rather than landed blind. Happy to open the (a)-producer + (a)-consumer as a focused PR if the approach looks right.

topIssues.title: app-core dist/entry.js externalizes un-hoisted transitive deps (jose, @node-rs/argon2) → ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND outside Bun's loader
topIssues.author: lalalune
topIssues.body:
## Summary

`packages/app-core`'s built `dist/entry.js` imports transitive runtime deps (`jose`, `@node-rs/argon2`, and likely more) as **external** packages. These deps live in Bun's content-addressable store but are not linked at the top level where Node-style resolution looks, so any command that runs `dist/entry.js` outside Bun's workspace-aware loader fails with `Cannot find module`.

Surfaced by a downstream QA smoke pass (milady-ai/milady#2179, finding #1 "layer 2").

## Repro

1. Build the app-core entry (`dist/entry.js`, ~7.6 MB across ~20 chunks).
2. Run it with a plain Node-style loader (e.g. via `doctor`, or any path that doesn't go through Bun's workspace loader).
3. First failure: `Cannot find module 'jose'`. Manually linking `jose` from Bun's store unblocks it, then it immediately hits `Cannot find module '@node-rs/argon2'`, and so on — whack-a-mole suggesting dozens more.

## Root cause

The bundler (tsdown) marks these runtime deps `external`, but they are transitive (not hoisted to the top-level `node_modules`), so Node-style resolution from `dist/entry.js` can't find them.

## Proposed fixes (pick one)

- (a) Bundle the needed transitive runtime deps **inline** in `dist/entry.js` (tsdown config: move them out of `external`).
- (b) Declare all needed runtime deps as **direct deps** of the package so they hoist to the top level.
- (c) Ship a runtime/loader that knows how to resolve into Bun's store.

## Severity

Medium — affects any command that runs `dist/entry.js` outside Bun's loader (e.g. `doctor`), and is broader than a single command.

topIssues.title: Release orchestrator startup_failure: missing platform-signing secrets
topIssues.author: lalalune
topIssues.body:
## Summary

Two release-orchestration workflows hit `startup_failure` on every tag push (most recently on `v2.0.3`):

- [Release All Platforms](https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26156834072) — `.github/workflows/release-all.yml`, triggered by `push: tags: [v*.*.*]`
- [elizaOS Full OS Release](https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26156833438) — `.github/workflows/elizaos-os-full-release.yml`, triggered by `release: types: [created]`

Both fail before any job runs (`This run likely failed because of a workflow file issue.`). YAML parses cleanly locally; all referenced reusable workflows exist at the tag.

## Root cause

Both orchestrators call reusable workflows whose `secrets:` blocks declare `required: true` for repo secrets that don't exist in `elizaOS/eliza`. When `secrets: inherit` can't supply a `required: true` secret, GitHub fails the parent at startup.

Confirmed via `gh secret list -R elizaOS/eliza` that these are missing:

| Secret | Required by | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `DEBIAN_GPG_PRIVATE_KEY` | `publish-apt-repo.yml` | sign apt repo |
| `DEBIAN_GPG_KEY_ID` | `publish-apt-repo.yml` | sign apt repo |
| `DEBIAN_GPG_PASSPHRASE` | `publish-apt-repo.yml` | sign apt repo |
| `CSC_LINK` | `release-electrobun.yml` (macOS sign) | Developer ID .p12 (base64) |
| `CSC_KEY_PASSWORD` | `release-electrobun.yml` | .p12 password |
| `APPLE_ID` | `release-electrobun.yml`, `apple-store-release.yml` | notarization Apple ID |
| `APPLE_APP_SPECIFIC_PASSWORD` | `release-electrobun.yml`, `apple-store-release.yml` | notarization |
| `APPLE_TEAM_ID` | `release-electrobun.yml`, `apple-store-release.yml` | Apple Developer Team ID |
| `ITC_TEAM_ID` | `apple-store-release.yml` | App Store Connect team ID |
| `APP_STORE_APP_ID` | `apple-store-release.yml` | numeric app ID |
| `MATCH_PASSWORD` | `apple-store-release.yml` | Fastlane Match passphrase |
| `MATCH_GIT_URL` | `apple-store-release.yml` | Fastlane Match repo |
| `WINDOWS_SIGN_CERT_BASE64` | `windows-store-release.yml` | Windows code-signing cert |
| `WINDOWS_SIGN_CERT_PASSWORD` | `windows-store-release.yml` | cert password |
| `WINDOWS_SIGN_TIMESTAMP_URL` | `windows-store-release.yml` | RFC 3161 timestamp server |
| `FLATHUB_TOKEN` | `flatpak-publish.yml` | declared `required: false`, not blocking |

The full secret list expected is documented in the header comment of `.github/workflows/release-all.yml`.

## Why the other release workflows worked

`NPM Release`, `Build Agent Image`, `Build elizaOS Linux ISO`, `Build elizaOS Debian Package` (build-only, not publish), `Release AOSP Flasher`, `Release Orchestrator`, `Snap Build & Test`, `Build & Release (Electrobun)`, `Release USB Installer` all started fine — their workflows don't `require: true` any missing secret. (USB Installer separately failed mid-run on a now-fixed `electrobun` PATH issue.)

## Resolution options

1. **Add the missing secrets** to `elizaOS/eliza` → Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions. This is the intended end state per the workflow header docs.
2. **Downgrade `required: true` → `required: false`** in the reusable workflows for platforms we don't intend to ship yet (e.g. if Windows Store / Apple Store / signed apt repo aren't in scope for v2.0.x). The leaf workflows already self-guard most calls.
3. **Stop calling unconfigured publish workflows** from `release-all.yml` until their secrets exist.

Probably want a mix: add Debian GPG (lowest-risk, internal-only) and Apple signing (highest-value), and gate Windows Store + Flathub behind explicit opt-in until those credentials are arranged.

## Context

- Tag for `v2.0.3` is live on `main` (`fa240156ed`). All other release workflows (NPM, Linux ISO, Android, Snap, Electrobun, etc.) ran or are running normally.
- Failed runs are also reproducible on the prior `v2.0.2` attempt — this is the pre-existing block, not a new regression.
- I don't have admin rights to download the run logs, which would confirm the exact secret-validation error message. Anyone with admin can drop the snippet here for the record.

topPRs.title: release: promote develop → main (Cloud V2 sync)
topPRs.author: standujar
topPRs.body:
## What this does

Standard develop → main promotion to sync prod with the recent Cloud V2 stack changes.

Once merged, the following auto-deploys will fire (per workflow path filters + env routing):

- **cloud-deploy-backend.yml** (env: production) → migrates prod Neon (no-op, already at 0135 per manual workflow_dispatch run today) + deploys cloud-api + Pages frontend
- **cloud-cf-deploy.yml** (env: production) → same
- **deploy-eliza-provisioning-worker.yml** (env: production) → SSHes eliza-1 (88.99.82.102), pulls main, restarts daemon
- **build-agent-image.yml** → builds + pushes ghcr.io/elizaos/eliza:stable + :latest

## Sanity already verified

- Prod Neon schema is already at the latest (0135) — manually migrated via workflow_dispatch earlier
- Staging stack is healthy on the same code (eliza-staging-1, cpx32 fsn1, Neon eliza-cloud-neon-dev)
- prod daemon heartbeats green for the past 1h+ (fleet-upgrade reconciler clean)

## Open items NOT blocking merge

- res.map warning in heartbeat publish on both envs (Upstash REST client quirk, non-blocking)
- Staging VM cloud-init template doesn't pre-install node 24 + cloud-shared symlink yet — fixed manually for current staging VM; needs follow-up to make next staging VM bootstrap clean

## DRAFT until

You give explicit go.

topPRs.title: feat(plugin-task-coordinator): odysseus-style orchestrator UI
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## Summary

Adds a full **chat-shell UI for the multi-agent orchestrator**, modeled on the
[odysseus](https://github.com/pewdiepie-archdaemon/odysseus) frontend (MIT) and
rebuilt from scratch in **React on `@elizaos/ui`**, wired to the **existing
elizaOS runtime**. This is **frontend only** — there is no new backend; all
task/session state continues to live in `@elizaos/plugin-agent-orchestrator`.

The new surface renders at **`/odysseus`** (and the existing **`/orchestrator`**
route) as a full-window app: a labeled session sidebar, a rich composer, a theme
engine, the memory/skills/notes/settings panels, and the full set of tool views.

> Credit: the original UI/UX design is from **odysseus** (MIT). This is an
> independent React reimplementation on elizaOS's own stack — none of the
> odysseus Python backend is used.

## What's included

**New `plugins/plugin-task-coordinator/src/odysseus/` shell (~40 files):**
- `OdysseusShell` root + `SessionSidebar` (folders, multi-select, pin, drag,
  resize) + collapsible 48px `IconRail` (mutually exclusive with the sidebar)
- `Composer` with slash-command + emoji autocomplete and a bottom action row
- Theme engine (`ThemeMenu` / `PresetsPanel`): preset recolor + customize
  (font / density / background), mapped onto elizaOS semantic CSS vars
- `MemoryPanel` (memories/skills/add/settings), `SkillsPanel`, `NotesPanel`,
  `SettingsPanel`
- Tool views: tasks, models, email, calendar, gallery (+ editor), compare,
  research, voice, group chat, cookbook, document library, admin
- A cross-view **window manager** (`WindowManager` + `useWindowControls`):
  drag / resize / edge-snap / minimize-to-dock (`MinimizedDock`)
- `useKeyboardShortcuts`, `useEscapeClose`, `useTaskRoom`, `useChatSubmit`

**Orchestrator render helpers:**
- `orchestrator-markdown` (via `marked`), `orchestrator-plan`,
  `orchestrator-reasoning`
- `orchestrator-stream` now always renders the user's own `stdin` messages
  (preserves the existing `senderKind === "user"` fix)

**App-shell `fullBleed` capability (`packages/ui`):**
- Opt-in `fullBleed?: boolean` on `AppShellPageRegistration`
  (backward-compatible). When set, the host shell mounts the page edge-to-edge
  via `ViewRouter` — **no header, no tab bar, no routed padding** — so a view
  that owns its whole window has no surrounding dashboard chrome.
- This **replaces the prior hardcoded `tab !== "orchestrator"` header
  special-case** in `RoutedShellContent` with a general, declarative flag.
- `/orchestrator` and the new `/odysseus` route are registered `fullBleed: true`.

**Other:**
- adds the `marked` dependency to the plugin
- isolates each `*.live.e2e.test.ts` suite's default chromium profile dir so
  parallel vitest file execution can't collide on the ProcessSingleton lock

## Verification

Built on a fresh branch off current `develop` and verified there:

- biome check — clean across all 53 changed source files
- `tsgo --noEmit` (plugin) — **0 errors** against current `develop` APIs
- `tsgo --noEmit` (`@elizaos/ui`) — 0 errors in `App.tsx` / `app-shell-registry.ts`
- plugin unit tests — **19/19** pass
- plugin build — `tsup` + views bundle + type declarations all succeed
- live e2e (`*.live.e2e.test.ts`) — **26/26** pass against a running dev stack,
  and Playwright render checks confirm the host header is absent on `/odysseus`
  (full-bleed) and the global chat overlay behaves correctly

## Scope / notes

- **Frontend only.** No actions/providers/services added; state stays in
  `@elizaos/plugin-agent-orchestrator`.
- The `fullBleed` flag is opt-in and changes no existing page's behavior.
- Some per-view actions render honest empty/disabled states where elizaOS has no
  corresponding client method yet (e.g. email IMAP/SMTP, calendar CalDAV,
  gallery image backend, voice TTS/STT) — these light up unchanged once the
  matching runtime routes exist. Happy to file follow-up issues for these.

Screenshots / a short screen recording can be added on request.

## Related

- Closes #8188 (proposal/motivation).
- Unblocked by #8187 — a develop-side `biome` format fix for `plugin-app-control/src/views/ViewManagerView.tsx` that currently red-fails the repo-wide **Format Check** gate (and every open PR). This PR's Format Check goes green once #8187 lands.

## CI status (for reviewers)

This PR's own gates are green: Format Check (this PR's files), Server Tests, unit-tests, lint-and-types, integration-tests, coverage on changed files, gitleaks, check-pr-title, route-coverage, iOS Simulator Build, Homepage Build, Electrobun Desktop Contract, Cloud Live E2E.

The currently-red lanes are **pre-existing on `develop`**, not introduced here — proven by #8187 (a 1-file format-only change) failing the *identical* set:

- Plugin Tests — `@elizaos/plugin-rlm` (`server.test.ts` spawns `/nonexistent/python`)
- Client Tests — `FineTuningTuiView.test.tsx` (plugin-training)
- Zero-Key Deterministic E2E, `bun run dev onboarding chat`, `env:audit:check`
- Android Debug Build — `shortcuts.xml` deep-link audit (`run-mobile-build.mjs`)
- Format Check (biome) — develop's `ViewManagerView.tsx` (→ #8187)

A short screen recording / screenshots of the live `/odysseus` surfaces can be added on request.

topPRs.title: refactor(lifeops): split plugin-lifeops into focused plugins + plugin-personal-assistant
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
## Summary

Decomposes the ~7.5K-LOC `plugin-lifeops` monolith into a slim orchestrator (`plugin-personal-assistant`) plus 6 new focused plugins, with the `/lifeops` dashboard view removed in favor of per-domain plugin views. Existing domain plugins (todos, health, calendar, documents, phone) gain the owner-facing actions and views they should have owned all along. Stub future homes for runtime primitives (owner-state, scheduled-task, registries, dispatch) are scaffolded in `@elizaos/core` and `@elizaos/app-core` to mark where the still-in-PA implementations will eventually migrate.

No backwards-compat shims — `@elizaos/plugin-lifeops` is gone, not aliased.

## What changed

### New plugins (Phase 1)
- **plugin-relationships** — entity/relationship graph (`ENTITY` action + Drizzle `app_relationships` schema). Absorbs lifeops `entities/`, `relationships/`, voice-observer bridge.
- **plugin-inbox** — cross-channel inbox (`INBOX` action + triage + Drizzle `app_inbox` + `/inbox` view). Distinct from plugin-messages (which stays Android-SMS-only).
- **plugin-goals** — life goals + self-care (`OWNER_GOALS`/`ROUTINES`/`REMINDERS`/`ALARMS` + `CheckinService` + Drizzle `app_goals` + `/goals` view).
- **plugin-blocker** — focus/distraction control (`BLOCK` action + website + app blocker services + `/focus` view).
- **plugin-finances** — owner finances (`OWNER_FINANCES` + Drizzle `app_finances` + `/finances` view).
- **plugin-remote-desktop** — `REMOTE_DESKTOP` action.

### Extended plugins (Phase 2)
- **plugin-todos** — `+ /todos` view (Today/Upcoming/Someday lanes); doc-block folds `OWNER_TODOS`.
- **plugin-health** — `+ OWNER_HEALTH`, `OWNER_SCREENTIME` actions; `+ /health` view (Sleep/Circadian/Screen-time/Activity/Connectors).
- **plugin-calendar** — `+ CALENDAR` (8 ops), `CONFLICT_DETECT` actions; `+ /calendar` view.
- **plugin-documents** — `+ OWNER_DOCUMENTS` action; `+ /documents` view.
- **plugin-phone** — `+ VOICE_CALL` (Twilio) action.

### Rename + view kill (Phase 3)
- `plugin-lifeops` → `plugin-personal-assistant` (727+50 file renames, 299 cross-workspace updates, zero remaining `@elizaos/plugin-lifeops` references).
- Internal exports renamed (`appLifeOpsPlugin` → `personalAssistantPlugin` etc.).
- `packages/shared/src/contracts/lifeops.ts` → `personal-assistant.ts`.
- App registry entry `apps/lifeops.json` → `apps/personal-assistant.json`.
- `/lifeops` view deleted (LifeOpsPageView, LifeOpsTuiView, XR variant, `lifeops.overview` widget). PA no longer ships a view — domain plugins do.
- `.github/workflows/lifeops-bench*.yml` filenames kept (benchmark-suite identifier, not the plugin name) — required-check refs would break.

### Core foundations (Phase 3, structural only)
Scaffold-only future homes; code still lives in `plugin-personal-assistant` until the next migration pass:
- `packages/core/src/owner-state/` — OwnerFactsStore, HandoffStore, GlobalPauseStore, FirstRunService, PendingPromptsStore interfaces.
- `packages/core/src/registries/` — Anchor, Gate, EventKind, EscalationLadder, Family, Blocker registry interfaces.
- `packages/core/src/scheduled-task/` — ScheduledTask types extending core's existing task-scheduler with gates/escalation/completionCheck/anchors.
- `packages/app-core/src/dispatch/` — ConnectorRegistry, ChannelRegistry, ApprovalQueue, send-policy interfaces.
Each module has a README with `TODO(migrate:)` pointing back to the still-canonical source.

### What stayed in `plugin-personal-assistant`
The cross-domain orchestration role:
- `BRIEF`, `PRIORITIZE`, `PERSONAL_ASSISTANT`, `SCHEDULED_TASKS`, `RESOLVE_REQUEST` actions.
- Default-pack composition (daily-rhythm, morning-brief, followup-starter, habit-starters, executive-assistant).
- The entity/relationship store, scheduled-task runner, owner-state, registries, connector/channel registries — all still here, will migrate into core/app-core in follow-up passes once the new homes exist.

## Test plan

- [x] `bun run lint` — exit 0
- [x] `bun run typecheck` — exit 0 workspace-wide
- [x] `bun run build` — exit 0 workspace-wide
- [ ] Manual smoke: load PA + the new domain plugins in dev-ui, confirm each registered view renders
- [ ] Manual smoke: `BRIEF` action still composes calendar + inbox + todos + finances correctly
- [ ] DB migration plan: new plugins introduce `app_relationships`, `app_inbox`, `app_goals`, `app_blocker`, `app_finances` schemas — verify their tables co-exist with PA's existing `app_lifeops` schema during the cut-over window

## Follow-up

This PR establishes the structural decomposition. The remaining migration moves the implementations out of `plugin-personal-assistant` into the new homes:
1. Move EntityStore + RelationshipStore + merge engine → plugin-relationships.
2. Move INBOX action body + TriageRepository → plugin-inbox.
3. Move OWNER_GOALS/ROUTINES/REMINDERS/ALARMS bodies + CheckinService + default-pack fragments → plugin-goals.
4. Move BLOCK + website-blocker/ + app-blocker/ → plugin-blocker.
5. Move OWNER_FINANCES body → plugin-finances.
6. Move CALENDAR/CONFLICT_DETECT bodies + calendar-gate + travel-time → plugin-calendar.
7. Move OWNER_DOCUMENTS body → plugin-documents.
8. Move OWNER_HEALTH/SCREENTIME bodies + ActivityTracker + PresenceSignalBridge → plugin-health.
9. Move ScheduledTaskRunner + Anchor/Gate/EventKind/EscalationLadder registries + OwnerState services → @elizaos/core.
10. Move ConnectorRegistry + ChannelRegistry + ApprovalQueue + send-policy → @elizaos/app-core.
11. After (1)-(10), PA becomes ~10% of its current size — pure BRIEF/PRIORITIZE/PA orchestration + default-pack composition.

topPRs.title: feat(orchestrator): add Odysseus workbench and operator inspector
topPRs.author: NubsCarson
topPRs.body:
## Current Status

This PR is intentionally **draft** and should not merge as one unit. It is the large Conductor/Odysseus operator-inspector stack and currently carries too much unrelated history for review or shipment.

Latest large-branch head: `0892cb7dc504c89c65d0c10f7ba25a5491812689` on `codex/conductor-inspector`.

Recommended ship path is the split PR stack:

- #8183 `chore: sync bun lock workspace entries` — lockfile-only base hygiene; fixes current workspace lock drift on `develop`
- #8184 `fix(app): restore dev smoke chat mount` — app/Vite dev-smoke fix stacked on #8183
- #8186 `fix(ci): align zero-key app smoke with startup shell` — CI-smoke drift cleanup stacked on #8184
- #8181 `test(orchestrator): cover ACP plan events and word-split text` — stacked on #8186
- #8182 `fix(orchestrator): guard stream identity and task deletion` — stacked on #8186
- Later: operator inspector drawer and Odysseus view/component port as separate reviewable PRs

## What This Stack Contains

- Odysseus-style orchestrator workbench surfaces and `/odysseus` routing from the local `nubs/orchestrator-view-feature` stack
- Conductor-inspired operator detail drawer concepts for sessions/timeline blocks with Input, Output, Events, and Usage tabs
- Stream identity hardening so rendered blocks remain traceable to message/event/session records
- Non-chat/full-bleed routing fixes so orchestrator/Odysseus controls are not covered by global chat chrome
- UI safety fixes including destructive-action confirmation and markdown URL sanitization
- Dev-only Vite/Bun CommonJS interop shims so local app dev smoke can mount the real chat UI again without changing production resolution
- API CORS support for `ELIZA_UI_PORT` so non-default Vite UI ports are accepted by the local API server

## Current Split-Stack Validation

- #8184: manual Dev Smoke passed, manual Cloud Tests passed, focused app/CORS/local smoke validations passed
- #8186: local startup/runtime tests, plugin-browser/elizaos tests, full assistant-home browser spec, reset-to-onboarding browser spec, ChatComposer unit coverage, scenario-runner PR coverage, Biome, and `git diff --check` passed
- #8186 fresh manual runs on head `e78a1b7f5e109771e41485df13d62879ad32d29c` are in progress:
  - Tests: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26839814087
  - Scenario PR E2E: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26839815886
  - Dev Smoke: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26839817661
  - Cloud Tests: https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/actions/runs/26839819151
- #8181 and #8182 are based on #8186 and are clean under `git diff --check origin/codex/ci-smoke-drift-cleanup...HEAD`; their focused tests passed before the final #8186 base-head advance and their diffs are unchanged

## Local Validation On The Large Branch

- `bunx biome check packages/app/src/shims/*.ts packages/app/vite.config.ts packages/app-core/src/api/server-cors.ts packages/app-core/src/api/server-cors.test.ts`
- `bunx tsc --noEmit --pretty false --skipLibCheck --ignoreConfig packages/app/src/shims/*.ts`
- `bunx vitest run packages/app-core/src/api/server-cors.test.ts` passed: 7 tests
- `bun run --cwd packages/app test -- test/route-coverage.test.ts test/ui-smoke-coverage.test.ts test/view-interaction-coverage.test.ts` passed: 19 tests
- Isolated UI probe confirmed `/chat` mounts, no page errors, and `chat-composer-textarea` is visible

## Notes

The Conductor study conclusion still stands: borrow Conductor's operator experience, not its deterministic DAG execution model. For Eliza, the immediate value is rendering and recovering from the DTOs the orchestrator already streams; a DAG editor, polling-worker queue model, retry matrix, and DLQ/compensation machinery should stay out unless product evidence says otherwise.

topPRs.title: Claude/agentic views surface
topPRs.author: lalalune
topPRs.body:
# Relates to # Risks # Background ## What does this PR do? ## What kind of change is this? # Documentation changes needed? # Testing ## Where should a reviewer start? ## Detailed testing steps

Greptile Summary

This PR introduces an **agent-surface framework** that makes every plugin view agent-controllable (registry, overlay, `useAgentElement` hook, and capability dispatch), plus a **view-action affinity layer** that upweights view-scoped runtime actions in the planner's prompt while the user is looking at that view. It also ships **sleep/wake lifecycle endpoints** for deep cold-storage of agent compute, **incremental diff backups** for agent sandboxes, and a **scheduled-backup cron** to keep agents continuously snapshotted. - Agent-surface: `ViewAgentRegistry`, `AgentSurfaceProvider`, `useAgentElement`, `AgentElementOverlay`, and capability handlers wired into both `DynamicViewLoader` and the new `ShellViewAgentSurface` wrapper. - Incremental backups: `agent-backup-diff.ts` computes field-level deltas; migration 0136 adds `backup_kind`/`parent_backup_id`/`content_hash` safely. - Sleep/wake: `executeSleep` backs up then frees compute; `executeWake` re-provisions and restores.

Confidence Score: 4/5

Safe to merge; the most consequential new paths are well-tested and the DB change is backwards-compatible. The three main feature areas all land with test coverage and the migration is safe. The pre-shutdown backup inconsistency leaves content_hash null on the most important backup rows, but is functionally safe since the column is nullable. packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/eliza-sandbox.ts — the executeSleep pre-shutdown backup path

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/agent/src/runtime/view-action-affinity.ts | New module mapping active plugin views to preferred runtime actions. | | packages/ui/src/agent-surface/registry.ts | New ViewAgentRegistry; AgentSurfaceProvider properly calls removeViewRegistry on unmount. | | packages/cloud-api/v1/eliza/agents/[agentId]/sleep/route.ts | New sleep endpoint; returns 202 for both new and already-in-progress jobs. | | packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/eliza-sandbox.ts | Adds executeSleep/executeWake. Pre-shutdown backup bypasses buildBackupInput, omitting content_hash. | | packages/cloud-shared/src/lib/services/agent-backup-diff.ts | New incremental backup diffing/patching library; logic is correct and well-tested. | | packages/cloud-shared/src/db/migrations/0136_agent_backup_incremental.sql | Adds backup_kind/parent_backup_id/content_hash columns safely with IF NOT EXISTS and DEFAULT 'full'. |

Sequence Diagram

```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Shell as Shell participant Registry as ViewAgentRegistry participant Affinity as view-action-affinity participant Optimizer as prompt-optimization participant Agent as Planner Shell->>Registry: getOrCreateViewRegistry() Shell->>Affinity: "setActiveViewContext({viewId})" Agent->>Shell: "capability=list-elements" Shell->>Registry: snapshot() Registry-->>Agent: elements[] Optimizer->>Affinity: getActiveViewContext() Affinity-->>Optimizer: activeView Optimizer->>Agent: prompt with Active View block ``` Reviews (3): Last reviewed commit: ["test(agent): cover character + automatio..."](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/commit/0424b6a57cf997545a078bd930228b2795715512) | [Re-trigger Greptile](https://app.greptile.com/api/retrigger?id=34774957)
